my_thoughts: The rejection/reassertion works: temporal measure (not linear time), reasserts: subjectivity (speed = quantity of impressions), defines: density as quantity (phenomenological). The student proposed temporal definition (testing temporal definition), which shows they understand: speed = linear time (temporal measure). They'll likely ask about correlation next.
accumulated_insight: T2: Student proposed temporal definition ('By speed of emotions do you mean the time between the cause and effect of an emotion?')—testing temporal definition via proposes linear definition and seeks temporal measure. They understand: speed = linear time (temporal measure). The comparison_contrast mechanism rejects: temporal measure (not linear time), reasserts: subjectivity (speed = quantity of impressions), defines: density as quantity (phenomenological) ('No. Speed of emotions means how many different feelings can pass through your emotional center in a given time. It is a subjective measure'). Pattern: measurement question → redefinition → temporal test → rejection/reassertion → [next: correlation question]. They're grasping: speed = quantity (not linear time), subjective (rejection/reassertion).
visible_response:
No, it is the quantity of impressions.
You put it objectively, but it is subjective;
it
means that in one thought one can have thousands of emotions.
